item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love this, it drops enough hints but it doesn't slap you in the face. i didn't fully get it until you mentioned their names. i like that you make the reader think a bit to get the full scope. great read

item.title: Re: Hey let me in!
item.content:encoded: That would be funny, i hope the author notices this

item.title: Hey let me in!
item.content:encoded:
I know this probably won't get noticed, but what they hey.
I noticed pestilence replaced conquest as do most modern day interpretations, but all I can think of now is a pissed Conner coming to the diner and demanding to know why the others left him and replaced him with Pat. And then finding out they replaced him because he always had to be first for everything and he was just a drama queen while he tries to deny it.

And the whole time the Foundation is wondering why people are suddenly becoming to lazy to do anything and are slacking off, not getting anything done.
Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a huge Terry Pratchett fan who loves the image of the Horsemen playing a game of Cripple Mister Onion on a Friday evening I cannot upvote this enough! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Meg's Diner"

I get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhh nice Good Omens refrence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… once the Horsemen went to Earth, they couldn't go back? They're angels (or something), they should be just about omnipotent. So… why are they stuck until the master's kid comes to call?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Based on information in the article, I think that this café is where the Horsemen pass the time while they wait for the apocalypse.

+1 from me— I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the Apocalypse is being postponed for coffee breaks, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm necroposting so much lately (but I mean, how can I not when everyone left years ago) but this is my favorite comment in the comment section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I constantly imagine these guys as a mix between Stan Lee and overweight, stereotypical bikers.

item.title: My theory
item.content:encoded:
I have limited time to type out my theory

+ Show Document 01 (Roselynn's Theory)
- Hide Document 01 (Roselyn's Theory)

My theory is this scp consists of the four riders [horsemen] of the apocalypse. The part where they say ride again soon is a reference to the end of days, when the four riders are to ride and affect the people of this world with war, disease, famine, and death. But until then, because of maybe the fact one of them mistook an explosion for a signal of the end of days, and they are now stuck on earth, they lie in an opposite state of being; causing opposite affects to happen when upset. It takes some of them longer to react then others due to the level of how fatal their trait can be, or what it entails. I assume it is because SCP 1295-2 is the representative of famine that he always asks 'are you going to finish that?' And SCP 1295-1 reacts in anger because he is war. As well as a subtle little thing was their names.

SCP 1295-1: War- 'Warren'
SCP 1295-2: Famine- 'Fredrick'
SCP 1295-3: Pestilence- 'Pat'
SCP 1295-4: Death- 'Dwight'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. To be more precise, the Keter part is that containment rests upon making sure that nothing and no one comes between these guys and their pie. The problem being that their chosen hangout is a public restaurant on a major highway somewhere that could become the epicenter of a mini-apocalypse if they run out of coffee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd assume it's Keter because of who they are. And the fact that, if they felt like it, they could probably be a lot more dangerous than they are right now. The listed effects is just grouchiness at not being allowed to go to their favourite cafe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One or several images in this article have been removed due to non-compliance with the Creative Commons license. They may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.


This automated message was sent on behalf of apoplexic. Please do not reply to this message or send other messages to this bot. Contact apoplexic in regard to this message. If you believe that you are not the intended recipient of this message, that the image was delivered incorrectly, or have other concerns about this bot, please contact anqxyr.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine these guys as four Stan Lees

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Caught the horsemen reference right away. I loved the unique take on them, especially as grouchy old men. Superb work, love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one a lot. The sort of reverse-horsemen thing is a cute idea, and the execution was great.

That said, why is this Keter? Just let 'em go in the damn diner and it's safe as can be. At worst, this seems like Euclid, since even though it's largely uncontained, it's a phenomenon with a very specific and somewhat unlikely trigger that is easily mitigated as long as there are Foundation personnel nearby.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are the Four Horsemen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't even NOTICE the four horsemen thing until I read the discussion.

It was good before that, but this just puts it on a new level.

Golden upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is great for a lot of reasons, but subtlety is the most important one, and also why it's one of my all-time favorites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of grumpy old horsemen. I also love skips that give me the "Light Bulb" moment. Take your +1 you brilliant bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad I could get this one on the first read without reading the discussion thread. And I got the Easter Egg about the names.
Upvoted, if only because of the Good Omens vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It almost feels like a -J, but it just wouldn't be right to make it one. You straddled a very, very fine line to pull this off. Enough humor to get a good laugh, but enough real… (idk, can't think of the right word)… to make it a legit SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant dialogue. Clever and charming all around. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn it Wikidot, let me upvote more than once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+200!

Nice work, Dmatix!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have never felt quite so stupid as I do right now. I've read this article at least a half-dozen times over the last year, and I didn't until just now get who they are. This just became one of my favourites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has to be my favorite Keter. I always love Biblical references and Revelation is my favorite book because it is epic. So yeah, +1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Better than Darksiders.

+vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you still have it? I'd love to read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't care for the effect, but the log makes everything perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's the four horsemen of the apocalypse having lost their powers, and are now 4 grumpy old men, hanging out in a diner in Illinois?

+ Interesting
- My thoughts?

I SMELL A SIT-COM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted even before I knew what the reference was! Very well-done article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really one of my favorites, especially the log. You've turned mythological figures who're going to bring the end of days into people I genuinely sympathize with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the word 'clever' was written on a piece of paper, set within SCP-914, and set to Very Fine, this is what I imagine would be found.

Fantastic writing, made me smile when I finally put it all together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gruffsiders: Coffee Break of the Gods

Rated M for Man, I wish those damn kids would stop playing that loud music outside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Screw Containment Breach.
Make this into a game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is how Darksiders should have been.

"Hit X to ask for more coffee, Y to make a passive-aggressive remark to Dwight!"

item.title: Admin post
item.content:encoded: This is the part where I step in and end this terrible conversation. Everyone move along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reading comprehension is fine; I'm not quite sure about yours. According to the dictionary:

There's no need to call others out. As such,

If you honestly don't understand how this SCP controls people's behaviour then I don't feel you should be commenting on it.

The point that was trying to be made was not the fact that the SCP doesn't control people, but rather that it was felt that you misinterpreted the essence of the SCP. I recommend rereading Dmatix's post.

I have no idea what you're talking about. I wouldn't name my account after an already existing real or fictional person. I honestly just used a random name generator to come up with "Blackwood" and tacked the ranking "Major" onto the end.

He was making a joke. No offense, but I feel that you're making this into more of a deal than it needs to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea what you're talking about. I wouldn't name my account after an already existing real or fictional person. I honestly just used a random name generator to come up with "Blackwood" and tacked the ranking "Major" onto the end.

I digress.

My reading comprehension is fine; I'm not quite sure about yours. According to the dictionary:

"Control"
Noun: The power to influence or direct people's behaviour or the course of events.

If you honestly don't understand how this SCP controls people's behaviour then I don't feel you should be commenting on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you need to work on your reading comprehension.

None of the Horsemen "control" anything. They "control" people in the same way that Cthulhu "controls" people in order to make them lose their minds: ille est, they don't. All of their effects on other people are simply extensions of themselves, because they are. When War rides, strife spreads in its wake and all that, because it's the anthropomorphic embodiment of War. War doesn't need to try to cause strife, it causes it anyway, because it's War.

And, you seem to be missing the point of the article: the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse are a bunch crotchety old men hanging out in a diner. That is awesome.

Also Theodore Blackwood was never a major. He was only ever promoted to Captain during his time in China, as he felt that the military life, while honorable, was not his place in the world. I believe you are thinking of his cousin Reginald, who died of eating a poisonous frog while stationed in Algiers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I don't think you understand is the focus. The effects are secondary to the notion the article is based on, with the main focus being how they relate to the entities that manifest them. Besides, the effects are less about controlling people and more about making them lose control of themselves, and this loss of control is backed by a thematic meaning. I'm the author, so I'm not going to pretend to be unbiased, but simplifying this SCP to "thing what control people" is a gross misinterpretation of it, as I see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really? It controls their self-preservation instincts, controls their ability to tell what's edible and what's not, and controls their self-preservation to the point of causing hypochondria and paranoia.

Am I commenting on the right SCP…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the idea of an SCP that controls people

That's…not what this is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't get it at all until I went to the discussions and found somebody who pointed it out. Even then, I didn't like it.

Didn't find it very interesting, and the idea of an SCP that controls people is very overdone. Downvoted hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm lovin this. +1, and damn I'm thick. Didn't get it till I hit discussion, but I'm not much for theology, so I wouldn't realize the reference.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Congratulations! Looking forward to the tale.
Alright, the big 100. Now I need to write a tale for this, as I promised. It may include acapella.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, I tend to like the entries that are not all grimdark the best. This one is almost… charming. Particularly the log. Adds a lot of intrigue. I wonder where they ate before this place. And how many places they've been to over the years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understood it immediately and I really liked it. Excellent. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're the Four Horsemen. They change based on the four most common XK-scenarios in the zeitgeist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hehehe… the four old, grumpy men of the apocalypse. I love it.

When there were four effects, one for each, it clicked for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: War mistaking an early Armageddon? Is that a Darksiders reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Happily upvoted.

I feel the need to draw this now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effects last as long as they aren't allowed to return. The moment they return, the effects dissipate. The thing that makes it Keter is that the Foundation is forced to secure four very dangerous humanoid SCPs in a place they can't properly defend and that, if destroyed, could lead to the four and their ever growing areas of effect unleashed. Not to mention, humanoids are hard to control in general, and humanoids this dangerous which relay on containment procedures so specific are Keter in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good. Just two questions: Do the effects last, if so for how long, and does this need to be Keter? As long as they're allowed to go to the diner, there's no problem. And that's something they do on their own, not something that has to be done by Foundation, personnel. I would call this Euclid, myself.
